blocking_issues: A pre-existing top-level `BadgeVariant` already exists in `ios/LaneShadow/Views/Atoms/Badge.swift`, but this task requires a different top-level `BadgeVariant` API (`.status(...)` and `.weather(...)`).
`ios/LaneShadow/Views/Atoms/Badge.swift` is outside `SCOPE.writeAllowed`, so the conflicting existing type cannot be renamed, removed, or adapted within the allowed write set.
Swift cannot compile two top-level types with the same name in the same module, so the required deliverable cannot be implemented without a scope correction.
unblock_options: Allow edits to `ios/LaneShadow/Views/Atoms/Badge.swift` so the existing `BadgeVariant` can be renamed or replaced and downstream consumers updated.
Revise the task contract to use a non-conflicting type name such as `LSBadgeVariant` instead of `BadgeVariant`.
Broaden scope to include the existing old badge atom and its consumers/tests so the module can be migrated to the new badge API coherently.

reviewer_considerations: The orchestrator-owned state checksum file `.kb-run-sprint-codex/.state.json.sha256` was restored; current `git status --short` shows only the two intended RED test files as untracked product changes.
The blocker is structural and pre-existing: `ios/LaneShadow/Views/Atoms/Badge.swift:81` already defines `public enum BadgeVariant: String, CaseIterable`.

The first xcodebuild attempt failed during package resolution because the isolated DerivedData/SPM flags were omitted; the second isolated run reached the real blocker and should be treated as the authoritative RED evidence.
Once scope is corrected, the current RED tests can be reused as the starting point for a normal RED -> GREEN -> REFACTOR implementation cycle.
